rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as moving beyond prior work that manipulated situational cognitive/emotional responses in self-regulation, and the cited paper is a prominent manipulation-based study of anticipatory self-control regret affecting indulgence vs. prudence choices. Both notes share core constructs (self-control dilemmas, indulgence/virtue tradeoffs, temporal perspective), making the cited work a plausible theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention. With only bibliographic matching and no in-text snippet, there is no clear evidence this is merely perfunctory.
